methods.name: SofT-GRPO
methods.source_status: official code and trained checkpoints available

checkpoints.status: official trained 1.5B checkpoint selected for the compute-compatible cross-method audit

evidence.claim: The sampler truncates and renormalizes a fixed top-k support, clamps sampled Gumbel noise to [-1.5, 3], forms the soft action, and sorts the stored ids and perturbed scores by executed weight.
Training reconstructs the executed mixture embedding from stored top-k ids and perturbed scores.

The latent objective renormalizes current probabilities on stored ids, clamps reconstructed Gumbel margins, applies a current-log-probability component mask, and averages the retained component terms.

methods.name: LEPO
methods.source_status: official code pinned; code predates paper v4; no public trained checkpoint found on 2026-07-14

evidence.claim: Transformers logits processors apply the configured TopK and TopP filters before Gumbel-Softmax; the resulting vector is executed as an embedding and its top-k entries are archived.
The default proxy id for a latent step is the clean processed-logit argmax.
The latent prefix uses advantage-weighted top-k soft cross entropy rather than a density ratio for the continuous action.
The soft-label loss sums archived top-k entries; under the released default those entries cover the sampler's entire nonzero support.
The released latent-32 evaluation example points to the public Qwen2.5-3B base model and only comments a local trained-checkpoint path.

methods.name: Latent-GRPO
methods.source_status: official code and trained checkpoints available

checkpoints.packaging_note: tokenizer adds compress_token at id 128256 while embedding and LM-head row count is 128256; audit prompts must not contain this out-of-range added token
checkpoints.status: official trained 1B checkpoint selected for the compute-compatible cross-method audit
official trained 7B checkpoint reserved for higher-compute replication

evidence.claim: The sampler constructs a dynamic top-p candidate set, clips Gumbels, then selects the perturbed top-k support.
The actor uses the stored perturbed scores to reconstruct the latent mixture embedding.
The released objective averages selected marginal Gumbel terms and applies an advantage-dependent straight-through gradient flip.

methods.name: RLPT
methods.source_status: paper-only prior-art audit; no official repository found on 2026-07-14

evidence.claim: The paper identifies truncated rollout versus full-vocabulary optimization as an off-policy mismatch for discrete-token LLM RL.
RLPT stores the behavior-policy Top-K mask and reuses it to compute masked current and old token probabilities on the same support.

methods.name: NF-CoT
methods.source_status: paper-only audit; project page exposed no code or model link on 2026-07-14

evidence.claim: The paper defines tractable normalizing-flow likelihoods for continuous thoughts and applies policy-gradient optimization in that latent space.

methods.name: Dropout-GRPO
methods.source_status: paper-only positive-control audit; the paper claims a reference implementation, but no official repository URL was discoverable on 2026-07-14

evidence.claim: The paper defines the rollout-shared Bernoulli mask as part of an augmented action and replays it from a stored RNG seed during the policy update.
The paper proves that its mean-centered group surrogate targets the mask-marginalized expected reward at the old policy, up to a finite-group scale factor.
